title: cerinthe glabra subsp. tenuiflora
intro: Cerinthe glabra subsp. tenuiflora, also known as hairy honeywort or blue shell flower, is a plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and promote relaxation. Cerinthe glabra subsp. tenuiflora can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world, including Europe and Asia.
